item.title: LIAM Alleges Average Medical Insurance Claim Only RM12,000
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
In defending the RM100k annual limit under the Base MHIT plan, LIAM claims that the average medical claim is only RM12,000, even though many ITOs in the insurance industry regularly tout policies with RM1m or higher annual limits for medical emergencies. The post LIAM Alleges Average Medical Insurance Claim Only RM12,000 appeared first on CodeBlue .

item.title: Malaysia Is Running Out Of Caregivers And Time — Rashidi Yahaya
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)

item.title: Health PSSC Moots Expansion Of Insurance Coverage For Mental Health Services
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The Health PSSC recommends expanding insurance coverage for mental health services, warning that limited coverage is pushing patients to pay out of pocket and straining public hospitals. Preventive care remains largely excluded from insurance. The post Health PSSC Moots Expansion Of Insurance Coverage For Mental Health Services appeared first on CodeBlue .

item.title: PARLIAMENT | AG-PP bill postponed; Hamzah says Anwar wants to vet international media
item.description:
Source: Malaysiakini (EN News)
What’s going on in Parliament today, with updates at 11.30am and 1.30pm.

item.title: IHH Healthcare Malaysia Marks Third Consecutive Year in Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 Rankings
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The continued recognition reflects governance systems that are firmly established across its hospitals, with patient safety and care standards applied consistently across specialties and services. The post IHH Healthcare Malaysia Marks Third Consecutive Year in Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 Rankings appeared first on CodeBlue .

item.title: Expert: Gradually Replace PTDs In MOH With Public Health, Allied Health Professionals
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
A public health medicine specialist (PHMS) wants a study to identify MOH's admin posts held by PTDs that can be gradually filled by PHMS or allied health professionals trained in health system management. PTDs are still relevant in some areas in HQ/state. The post Expert: Gradually Replace PTDs In MOH With Public Health, Allied Health Professionals appeared first on CodeBlue .
